Template for memory allocation functions with customizable type names.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares three external functions related to memory management for a specific type, indicated by the placeholder `${name}`. The function `_applib_type_malloc_${name}` is intended to allocate memory for an object of the specified type. The function `_applib_type_zalloc_${name}` is similar but initializes the allocated memory to zero. The function `_applib_type_size_${name}` returns the size of the type in bytes. These declarations suggest that the actual implementations of these functions are defined elsewhere, and they are used to manage memory for a custom data type.
